_meta.scope: Public landing page only (/). Team-area pages remain in English by design.
_meta.note: This is the canonical source. de.json and it.json mirror these keys.

index.page_title: TERRRA Europa — Environmental restoration & project development
index.meta_description: TERRRA Europa UG is a Hamburg-based environmental project development and consulting company supporting ecosystem restoration, regenerative land use, and EU research collaboration.
index.stealth_tag: Environmental restoration · Project development · Research cooperation
index.hero_sub: TERRRA Europa is an environmental project development and consulting company. We support landowners, public bodies, universities and NGOs in restoring marginal European lands — through feasibility studies, project design, scientific monitoring, and EU research cooperation.
index.contact_label_accent: Let us know how we can help.
index.submit_success: ✓ Thank you. We will reply by email when relevant.
index.submit_error_invalid: Please enter a valid email address.
index.submit_error_generic: Something went wrong — please email us directly at info@terrra-europa.com
index.consent_label: I consent to TERRRA Europa storing my email address and selected role for the purpose of replying to my enquiry, in accordance with the {privacy_open}privacy policy{privacy_close}.
index.pillar1_body: Feasibility studies, project design, and implementation planning for environmental restoration and regenerative land-use projects. We work with landowners, public bodies, and farming partners across Europe.
index.pillar2_body: Independent, sensor-supported environmental monitoring of restoration outcomes — soil, water, and vegetation indicators. Scientific-grade data aligned with EU environmental reporting standards, for project owners, public authorities, and research partners.
index.pillar3_body: Coordination of grant-funded research and innovation projects, including EU research programmes, in cooperation with universities, public authorities, NGOs, and private-sector partners.
index.what_we_do_intro: TERRRA Europa supports the design, scientific monitoring, and coordination of environmental restoration projects in Europe.
index.service1: Sustainability and land-use consulting for landowners, public bodies and NGOs
index.service2: Feasibility studies and project design for restoration and regenerative-agriculture projects
index.service3: Independent environmental monitoring (soil, water, vegetation) and reporting
index.service4: Implementation planning, partner coordination, and participation in European research, innovation and grant-funded programmes
index.service5: Cooperation with agricultural partners, universities, and research institutions
index.footnote_badge: HARMONY · EIC Pathfinder Open 2026 (proposal submitted, not awarded)
index.footnote_text_html: TERRRA Europa is listed as a proposed partner in a submitted HARMONY consortium application to EIC Pathfinder Open 2026 (proposal ID 101353239, decision expected Q4 2026). The consortium proposes sensor-driven MRV for warm-climate peatlands across 8 institutions in 5 countries. This is a submitted proposal — funding has not been awarded.
index.revenue_model: Our revenue comes from project-development services, scientific monitoring contracts, sustainability consulting, and EU research funding. We provide technical, scientific and consulting services only; we do not participate economically in any environmental certificate, credit or allowance proceeds, and do not act as broker, custodian, or transfer agent for such instruments.
index.compliance_note: TERRRA Europa develops and monitors ecosystem restoration projects. We do not issue, register, broker, sell, assign, tokenize, or receive proceeds from carbon credits, biodiversity credits, or emission allowances; nor do we operate a marketplace, exchange, brokerage, trading platform, or financial service.
index.footer_company: TERRRA Europa UG (haftungsbeschränkt) i. G. · Moorfuhrtweg 4, 22301 Hamburg · Germany
index.cookie_text: We use a strictly necessary cookie to remember your language. We'd also like to use Google Analytics to understand site usage — your choice.
